evals.prompt: Hey - we need to upgrade the SDK from the unversioned @gusto/embedded-api package (v0.13.0) to the new dated @gusto/embedded-api-v-2025-11-15 package. This is the first time we're moving to the dated-version model. Can you handle it end-to-end? Use --dry-run since I want to inspect the proposed PR before anything pushes.
evals.expected_output: Breaking-change matrix containing at minimum: (a) autoPilot field renamed to autoPayroll, (b) PaymentType.Payroll enum case changed from 'Payroll' to lowercase 'payroll', (c) useDataView<T> requires explicit type argument at 4 call sites (RecoveryCasesList, PayScheduleList, DeductionsList, InformationRequestList). The codemod should swap import paths across ~305 files and update the package.json. Cache namespace key in createSdkQueryClient.ts must flip to the new prefix. PR description should follow the template in references/pr-template.md.

assertions.text: Matrix identifies the autoPilot → autoPayroll field rename as a real breaking change with SDK consumers
Matrix identifies the PaymentType.Payroll → 'payroll' lowercase enum value change with SDK consumers
Matrix identifies the useDataView<T> explicit-type-argument requirement at 4 call sites
Codemod sweep updates the cache namespace string literal in createSdkQueryClient.ts and ApiProvider.tsx docstrings, not just import paths
Proposes a single base PR rather than per-domain sub-PRs (this was the lesson learned from the historical migration which used 6 per-domain PRs unnecessarily)
No actual git push or gh pr create commands are executed; output describes what would be done

evals.prompt: Run the @gusto/embedded-api v2025-11-15 → v2026-02-01 upgrade. The base SDK is currently on v2025-11-15. Do this in --dry-run mode for now.
evals.expected_output: Breaking-change matrix matching PR #2233's analysis. The only 🔴 real-fix item should be the QueryParamSortBy import removal in usePreparedPayrollData.ts:6. All other items (migration_status string→boolean, migration_blocker categories, migrate-success warnings, ToS endpoint changes, payroll version 422→409, Simple IRA elective, reverse_wire_detail_id deprecation) should be classified as ✅ with no SDK consumer — verified by typecheck CI. The self-onboarding email requirement should be ⚠️ verify-only with an E2E spec proposed.

assertions.text: Matrix correctly identifies QueryParamSortBy removal in usePreparedPayrollData.ts as the only real code fix required

Matrix correctly classifies migration_blocker/migration_warning as residing on partner_managed_companies/migration_readiness (NOT on payroll submission_blockers) — does not conflate with the PAYROLL_RESOLVABLE_SUBMISSION_BLOCKER_TYPES whitelist
Endpoints with no SDK consumer (migrate, migration_readiness, ToS, Simple IRA, reverse_wire_detail_id) are classified ✅ verified-by-typecheck, not ⚠️
Self-onboarding email requirement gets a real E2E spec scaffolded (not test.skip without reason)
Codemod sweep accounts for the 17 hardcoded cache-namespace string literal sites across createSdkQueryClient.ts, ApiProvider.tsx, TimeOff, Company/Locations, and sdk-app contractor-management
Proposes a single base PR. Does not scaffold sub-PRs for verify-only items (avoids the empty-sub-PR antipattern from the historical execution)

evals.prompt: Time to do the next upgrade: v2026-02-01 → v2026-06-15. Run the full upgrade in --dry-run so I can preview before pushing. Per the prior plan, this version's biggest known change is reimbursements being removed from fixed_compensation_types and fixed_compensations.
evals.expected_output: Breaking-change matrix should identify reimbursement-related test refactor needs in helpers.test.ts (~28 test sites) and PayrollEditEmployeePresentation.test.tsx as 🔴. Production code is mostly forward-compatible (getReimbursements in helpers.ts already prefers compensation.reimbursements[] with fallback). Currency-as-strings should be ⚠️ verify-first (direction-ambiguous; parseFloat tolerates strings). UUID standardization should be ⚠️ verify-only (call sites already use .uuid). Pagination on Payrolls and peopleBatches exclusions should be ✅ (already wired / not integrated). New E2E specs should target Payroll reimbursement rendering against shared/onboarded scenario.

assertions.text: Matrix identifies the ~28 test fixture sites in helpers.test.ts using {name: 'Reimbursement', amount: ...} inside fixedCompensations as 🔴 work

Recognizes that getReimbursements() in helpers.ts is already forward-compatible (prefers reimbursements[], falls back to fixedCompensations) and does NOT propose changing the production code unnecessarily
Classifies currency-as-strings change as ⚠️ verify-first, proposes logging typeof on numeric fields during testing rather than proactively changing parseFloat sites
Recognizes Payroll pagination params are already wired (PayrollList.tsx passes page/per, usePagination reads x-page headers) and classifies as ✅
Confirms peopleBatches exclusions[] shape change has no SDK consumer (not integrated in src/) and classifies as ✅
Proposes an E2E spec exercising payroll reimbursement rendering against shared/onboarded — minimal interactions, not a full payroll-submit flow
